description_long: This mosque features a large rectangular structure with a spacious central courtyard surrounded by arcaded walkways and a prominent minaret rising from one corner. The exterior walls are plain and light-colored, punctuated by arched doorways and small windows, while the roof is mostly flat with a muted green hue. The minaret is tall and square, adorned with decorative bands and topped with a finial of stacked spheres. The main entrances are marked by ornate, arched portals with subtle decorative elements. The courtyard is paved and open, bordered by colonnades with wooden awnings and arches. The overall design is simple yet monumental, emphasizing geometric forms and symmetry typical of North African Islamic architecture.
description: A large, rectangular mosque with a central courtyard, flat green roof, and a tall, square minaret featuring decorative bands and a finial. The structure is defined by arched entrances, plain light walls, and arcaded walkways.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main courtyard, the arcaded walkways, and the tall minaret with its decorative finial on the left side.
description_view_1: From this angle, the flat green roof dominates, with the minaret visible at the back and the main entrance archway centered in the foreground.
description_view_2: This side view shows the minaret in profile, the arched entrance, and the plain expanse of the mosque's exterior wall.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the symmetry of the courtyard and arcades, with the minaret on the left and the main entrance archway at the center.
attribute_minaret: One minaret, tall and square, located at a corner of the mosque, rising significantly above the main structure, with decorative bands and a finial of stacked spheres.
attribute_islamic_geometric_patterns: None visible; exterior surfaces are mostly plain with minimal ornamentation.
attribute_architectural_style: Moroccan/Western Islamic, characterized by a square minaret, arcaded courtyard, flat roof, and simple geometric forms.
